purposes.description: Compute bus voltage angles and line flows using the DC power flow approximation (B-matrix linear system solve).

primitives.evidence: Requires numpy (np.linalg.solve, np.zeros, np.concatenate) to build and solve the B-matrix linear system.
Multi-step computation: susceptance from reactance, building B-matrix with off-diagonal terms, computing net injections.
Output must conform to specific JSON schema (power_flow.json with bus_angles, line_flows, generation arrays).
Must write and execute Python script to perform numerical computation.
Multiple constraints: slack bus angle must be 0, remove slack row/column, correct sign convention for flows, correct B-matrix indexing.

purposes.description: Solve economic dispatch to minimize total generation cost using lambda iteration (equal incremental cost method) with generator limits.

primitives.evidence: Requires numpy for numerical computation; implements bisection-based lambda iteration algorithm.
Compound reasoning: quadratic cost curves, marginal costs, bisection search, clamping to generator limits, total cost aggregation.
Optimization under constraints: minimize cost subject to power balance and generator min/max limits.
Output must conform to dispatch.json schema with per-generator dispatch, total_cost, and lambda.
Must write and execute Python script for iterative numerical optimization.

purposes.description: Verify that computed line flows do not exceed thermal limits and identify overloaded lines.

primitives.evidence: Simple loop comparing absolute flow values against limits using standard Python.
Single-step comparison: absolute value of flow vs. limit.
